title: Man charged with boating under the influence after Mass. college student drowns in Connecticut
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Michael Collins charged with boating under the influence after a fatal canoe accident in East Haddam, Connecticut.
Victim Dominick Tocci, a College of the Holy Cross student, drowned after canoe overturned on Lake Hayward.
Collins released on a promise to appear in court on July 23 following his arrest by Connecticut police.

title: Nine years after South End killing, fugitive wanted on murder charge captured in North Carolina
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Fugitive Alan Marcus Lewis, wanted for a 2017 South End murder, was captured in Charlotte, North Carolina.
Lewis allegedly killed Brent Stevenson in a 2017 shooting; the incident led to a fatal car crash in Boston.
The U.S. Marshals located Lewis after a multi-year search; he had been living under assumed identities.

title: The people keeping Cape Cod’s car culture alive
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cape Cod Classics hosts over 20 events yearly, attracting nearly 500 members and raising over $11,000 for charity.
Smaller clubs like the Cape Cod British Car Club focus on specific themes, like classic British cars and local cruises.
Heritage Museums & Gardens volunteers restore historical cars, bringing 32 of 43 vehicles back to operational status.

title: Amtrak warns customers that trains may experience delays this week due to ‘extreme temperatures’
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Amtrak warns of possible train delays in Northeast due to extreme heat from July 15 to July 17.
Trains may operate at reduced speeds during high temperatures, standard practice when tracks exceed 128 degrees.
Customers are advised to monitor train status on the Amtrak website or app for updates during heat advisory.

title: Elin Hilderbrand talks ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ Nantucket, and her next book
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in Peacock's adaptation of Elin Hilderbrand's novel 'The Five-Star Weekend,' filmed on Nantucket.
Elin Hilderbrand actively participated in the TV adaptation, providing insights into filming locations and island authenticity.
Hilderbrand's next projects include a sequel, 'The Thoroughbreds,' co-written with her daughter, releasing on September 15.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (July 15)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington homes sold include a one-family Colonial on Old Farm Road for $1,225,000 and a 1928 bungalow for $550,000.
Acton's top property was a 2012 Colonial on Woodchester Drive, selling for $2,170,000, with several Great Road condos at $1,380,000.
Arlington's highest sale was a 1936 Colonial on Churchill Ave for $1,980,000, with a 1915 old-style home on Paul Revere Road at $899,000.

title: Active-duty Everett firefighter dies from occupational cancer after 30 years of service, department says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett firefighter John Benoit, 63, dies of occupational cancer after over 30 years of service.
Benoit, a Wilmington resident, continued a family firefighting legacy after serving in the Air Force.
Benoit's wake is at JF Ward Funeral Home, Everett, followed by a funeral Mass at Our Lady of Grace Church.

title: 2 Dorchester women charged in alleged human trafficking scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Yuberkys Recci and Keysha Ortiz from Dorchester were charged with human trafficking, kidnapping, and multiple firearm offenses.
Recci allegedly coerced a homeless victim into sex work, confiscated her earnings, and held her captive in multiple locations.
Authorities linked the suspects to the crime through social media and GPS data; both were held without bail pending a hearing.

title: ‘Brilliant, unique soul’: Mother remembers Fall River teen who drowned in pond
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tristan Major, 17, drowned at South Watuppa Pond in Fall River on July 12; his body was recovered by police divers.
Mother Kailyn Lord described her son Rohan Rhys as intelligent and unique, with future plans including a therapeutic horse ranch.
Fall River Public Schools offered counseling after the incident; authorities do not suspect foul play in the drowning.

title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video of a karaoke night at Gaslight in Nantucket, recorded on September 30, 2025.
The video features Garner and "The Five-Star Weekend" co-stars singing ABBA's "Dancing Queen" with local guide Bill Jamieson.
Garner expressed fondness for the experience while filming in Nantucket and praised the island's attractions and activities.
